research_series.series_name: Missing Data, Partial Observability, Uncertainty, and Sparse Multimodal Health Inference

patterns.pattern_name: Sparse or missing multimodal data requires explicit uncertainty and missingness modeling

mediator.name: missingness_mechanism_and_uncertainty
mediator.mechanism: MCAR/MAR/MNAR/device nonwear/selective reporting require different imputation and confidence handling

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Absence of a signal is not evidence of absence when data are missing, noisy, sparse or selectively reported; high-confidence latent-state inference requires convergent independent observations.

hypotheses.reason: Missingness mechanisms and imputation uncertainty strongly influence prediction validity.

evidence.summary: Multimodal fusion can improve prediction, but missingness mechanisms, imputation uncertainty and lack of validation can bias latent-state inference.

key_sources.title: Inferring Hidden Physiological States from Incomplete Multimodal Health Data
key_sources.relevant_finding: Do not infer hidden states with high confidence from sparse signals unless multiple independent observations converge; explicitly mark MCAR/MAR/MNAR/device nonwear/selective reporting.
key_sources.population: human digital health and clinical prediction studies

limitations: Many ML prediction models have high risk of bias

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: One imputation method solves missingness in real-world health data
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Reviews agree methods must be tailored to missingness mechanism and data structure.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: A guardrail preventing missing/unmeasured data from being interpreted as normal or absent physiology.
Multiple independent signals supporting the same latent-state hypothesis.

research_gaps: Need more validation of uncertainty-aware latent state inference for fatigue, brain fog and recovery using wearables+EMA+labs.

most_supported_patterns: Complete-case deletion biases nonrandom missing data.
Multimodal ML outperforms unimodal prediction when independent signals converge.
Uncertainty quantification is essential but underused outside imaging.
highest_value_patterns_for_inference: Separate missing, normal, noisy and confounded signals.
Require convergent evidence for high-confidence latent states.
recommended_next_queries: How to define uncertainty scoring for BodyState Mapper outputs?
summary.overall_assessment: Essential partial-observability layer for sparse real-world data.

works_cited.title: Inferring Hidden Physiological States from Incomplete Multimodal Health Data
